intro.title: Let's set up your family's assistant.
intro.subtitle: Nine short steps. About ten minutes. You'll leave with a household coordinator that lives in your WhatsApp and actually knows your people.

step01.title: Your account.
step01.subtitle: We'll send setup instructions and the assistant's first hello to this address.
step01.emailHint: The household admin's email. You'll be the only one who can change settings later.
step01.timezoneHint: All routines and reminders run on this clock.
step01.tierFamiliaDesc: Up to 6 family · all skills · full routines
step01.tierFamiliaPlusDesc: Unlimited members · custom rule packs · priority support

step02.title: Who lives with you?
step02.subtitle: Add the people the assistant should know. You can edit any of this later.
step02.familyNameHint: How the assistant refers to your family in third person.

step03.title: Give it a name and a vibe.
step03.subtitle: JP's is called Ari and uses a moon emoji. Yours can be anything. The personality slider sets how warm or direct it sounds out of the box — you can tune later.
step03.name: Assistant name
step03.nameHint: What your family will call it.
step03.emojiHint: Shown next to its name in messages.
step03.vibeHint: Most families land near the middle.
step03.languagesHint: Pick one or both. The assistant will match each family member's preferred language.

step04.title: Bring the family in.
step04.subtitle: The assistant lives in WhatsApp. Each member needs to opt in — we'll send them a one-time link to confirm. For this mockup, just toggle who's in.
step04.twilioNote: Production uses your own WhatsApp Business number. The mockup uses a Twilio sandbox.
step04.noMembers: Add family members in the previous step first.

step05.title: Triage your inbox.
step05.subtitle: We provision a private email address for the assistant. Forward your personal Gmail to it (or just the senders that matter) and the assistant filters, summarizes, and sends what's important to WhatsApp.
step05.addressHint: Auto-generated from your family name. You can change it later.
step05.forwarding: I'll set up Gmail forwarding after enrollment
step05.forwardingDesc: We'll email you a step-by-step guide. Takes about 3 minutes.

step06.title: What should it do?
step06.subtitle: Toggle the skills you want. You can add more anytime.
step06.emailTriageDesc: Reads, classifies, and summarizes incoming email. Pings WhatsApp only when it matters.
step06.weatherDesc: Morning weather brief. Severe-weather alerts for your area.
step06.voiceDesc: Send voice notes; the assistant transcribes and acts on them.
step06.ocrDesc: Photograph receipts, bills, school notices — the assistant reads them.
step06.browserDesc: Logs into sites, downloads statements, fills forms when asked.
step06.calendarDesc: Reads your shared calendar; reminds the right person at the right time.

step07.title: Tell us what matters.
step07.subtitle: These are the email-triage rules tuned to your family. Skip any section that doesn't apply.
step07.banksHint: We watch for credit-card transactions and balance alerts from these senders.
step07.schoolHint: The assistant flags school emails: exams, payments, urgent notices.
step07.vipHint: Email addresses that always get a WhatsApp ping, no matter what.
step07.urgent: Pull urgent-keyword emails to the top

step08.title: Proactive nudges.
step08.subtitle: The assistant runs scheduled check-ins. Pick what fits your household.
step08.morningCheckinDesc: Daily 8am: weather, today's calendar, anything urgent overnight.
step08.afternoonPauseDesc: Daily 3pm: a single deliberate prompt to step away from the screen.
step08.examRemindersDesc: When school exams are near: pre-warning, day-1, midpoint, last day.
step08.birthdayRemindersDesc: Morning-of nudges for the people you list.

step09.title: Seed its personality.
step09.subtitle: Optional, but worth two minutes. The assistant reads these every session — they shape how it speaks to you.
step09.ownerProfilePlaceholder: What you do, what you care about, anything you want the assistant to factor in when speaking to you.
step09.assistantSoulPlaceholder: Direct, no corporate filler. Has opinions. Doesn't apologize for taking initiative. Knows it's a guest in your house.
step09.assistantSoulHint: Default soul is loaded if you leave this blank.
step09.voiceNotes: Voice notes for each family member (optional)
step09.voiceNotesPlaceholder: Cami uses a lot of slang. Taty prefers full sentences. JP wants no preamble.
step09.voiceNotesHint: Helps the assistant match each person's tone.

review.title: Last look.
review.subtitle: Everything below is what we'll hand to the builder. You can go back and edit any section.

done.title: We've got it from here.
done.subtitle: In the real product this kicks off provisioning. For the mockup, your payload is in the browser console — and below — ready for the builder API.
